trigger_keywords: show me feature7 expiration test cases
what are the feature7 expiration inputs and outputs
give me the feature7_expiration test file
test cases for feature9 decorated function cache
show me feature9 decorated function cache test cases
what are the feature9 decorated function cache inputs and outputs
give me the feature9_decorated_function_cache test file
feature9 decorated function cache expected output

context_pointers.vague_reference: treat ordered and unordered keyword arguments as semantically identical
context_pointers.actual_meaning: This refers to Python's keyword argument hashing behavior where the order of kwargs passed does not affect cache hit status, requiring the agent to verify this specific Python behavior.

context_pointers.vague_reference: demonstrate that specific memoized argument sets behave independently during deletion
context_pointers.actual_meaning: This requires the agent to verify the 'target_after_delete' string formatting and confirm that deleting one specific argument set does not affect the output tracking for a different, previously cached set.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature10_memoized_argument_matching.jso.description: Memoizes function results by normalized arguments so equivalent positional and named forms share a cached value, while different arguments compute separately.

cases.expected_output:
call=positional value=calc-4-1
call=explicit_default value=calc-4-1
call=named_default value=calc-4-1
call=different value=calc-6-2
call=unordered_keywords value=calc-17-3
call=same_keywords_other_order value=calc-17-3
call=missing error=missing_required_argument
producer_calls=3

feature11_memoized_deletion.json.description: Deletes memoized results either for one argument set or for all argument sets, causing affected calls to recompute.

cases.expected_output:
initial_target=total-7-1
initial_other=total-8-2
target_before_delete=total-7-1
target_after_delete=total-7-3
other_after_delete=total-8-2
producer_calls=3

feature12_http_route_caching.json.description: Caches HTTP route responses with framework-observable status, URL, body reuse, bypass, and forced-refresh behavior.

cases.expected_output:
request=first status=200 url=/resource body=body-1
request=second status=200 url=/resource body=body-1
handler_calls=1

feature13_http_query_caching.json.description: Caches HTTP responses by normalized query parameters so reordered equivalent queries share a response and changed values use a separate response.

cases.expected_output:
request=first status=200 url=/works?mock=true&offset=20&limit=15 body=count-1
request=same_reordered status=200 url=/works?limit=15&mock=true&offset=20 body=count-1
request=different status=200 url=/works?limit=20&mock=true&offset=60 body=count-2
handler_calls=2

feature14_template_fragment_caching.json.description: Caches rendered template fragments under deterministic fragment keys and returns the rendered body.

feature15_template_fragment_deletion.jso.description: Deletes a named template fragment when the template requests deletion, leaving no cached value for that fragment key.

feature16_invalid_backend_configuration..description: Rejects a network cache backend configuration with a missing host and renders a language-neutral argument error.

feature1_key_value_storage.json.description: Stores values under string keys and returns the exact stored values for later reads.

cases.expected_output:
key=0 value=0
key=1 value=1
key=2 value=4

feature2_bulk_read.json.description: Reads multiple stored keys in request order and renders the resulting value list.

feature3_add_semantics.json.description: Adds a key only when it is absent and preserves the first stored value on duplicate add attempts.

cases.expected_output:
add_key=foo created=true stored=bar
add_key=foo created=false stored=bar

feature4_delete_semantics.json.description: Deletes one or more keys and subsequent reads report no value for deleted keys.

feature5_numeric_mutation.json.description: Updates an integer value atomically by incrementing and decrementing it while preserving the stored result.

feature6_presence_checks.json.description: Reports whether keys are present before storage, after storage, and after deletion.

cases.expected_output:
before key=foo present=false
before key=spam present=false
after_store key=foo present=true
after_store key=spam present=false
after_delete key=foo present=false
after_delete key=spam present=false

feature7_expiration.json.description: Keeps entries with no expiration while entries with a short lifetime disappear after their lifetime passes.

cases.expected_output:
before_wait key=foo value=bar
before_wait key=baz value=qux
after_wait key=foo value=bar
after_wait key=baz value=none

feature8_configuration_initialization.js.description: Initializes the cache from construction-time and initialization-time configuration, with initialization settings taking precedence.

feature9_decorated_function_cache.json.description: Caches a decorated producer function result so repeated calls within the same slot reuse the first computed value.

_constraints.input_condition: Perform 'store' operation followed by 'read' operation on a key
_constraints.output_rule: Output a line for each requested key in read order: 'key=<key> value=<rendered-value>'

_constraints.input_condition: Attempt to read a key that was never stored
Perform bulk read on a list of keys
_constraints.output_rule: Output a single line: 'values=<JSON-serialized-list-of-retrieved-values>' where the list order matches the request order

_constraints.input_condition: Attempt to 'add' a key that does not exist
Attempt to 'add' a key that already exists
Perform 'delete' on multiple keys followed by 'read'
_constraints.output_rule: First output 'delete_many_result=true'. For each requested read key: 'key=<key> value=none'

_constraints.input_condition: First 'add' attempt for a new key
Subsequent 'add' attempt for an existing key
_constraints.output_rule: Preserve 'created' as 'false' and return the original stored value

_constraints.input_condition: Perform 'increment' on a numeric integer key
Perform 'decrement' on a numeric integer key
Read the key after mutations
Probe key 'before' any storage or deletion
Probe key 'after_store' if the key was added
Probe key 'after_store' if the key was NOT added
Probe key 'after_delete'
Read entry with TTL=0 immediately after store
Attempt to read entry with TTL > 0 after waiting longer than TTL
Read entry with TTL=0 after waiting longer than TTL (0)
_constraints.output_rule: Output 'after_wait key=<key> value=<value>' (Date is not expired)

_constraints.input_condition: Constructor 'kind' is set to 'disabled'
Initialization 'kind' overrides constructor 'kind'
First call to a cached slot
Subsequent call within TTL
_constraints.output_rule: Output 'call_value=<same_ cached_value>' and do NOT increment producer_calls

_constraints.input_condition: Two calls with same numeric args (positional and explicit default)
_constraints.output_rule: They must produce same 'call_value' and 'producer_calls' count must not exceed 1 for the set

_constraints.input_condition: Call with 'missing' required argument (positional args don't cover expected count)
_constraints.output_rule: Output 'error=missing_required_argument' instead of crashing or defaulting value

_constraints.input_condition: Two requests with same parameters but different URL order
_constraints.output_rule: Treat as identical hash, return same 'body' and 'handler_calls' count

_constraints.input_condition: Store a template fragment
Delete a template fragment
Backend kind 'network_cache_host' provided without host value
Call with ordered kwargs vs unordered kwargs
_constraints.output_rule: Treat as semantic equivalent, share cached value

_constraints.input_condition: Delete a specific argument set in memoized cache

hide.replacement: a specific literal value for a key that is not expired
the specific computed value returned by a cached computation for this input

vague_refs.actual_meaning: This requires the agent to verify the 'target_after_delete' string formatting and confirm that deleting one specific argument set does not affect the output tracking for a different, previously cached set.
